candidate_problems.text: How can a self-referential legitimacy loop be made operational and measurable without collapsing legitimacy into proxy metrics? The conjecture depends on detecting perception, comparing it to purpose, and revising rules, but it leaves unresolved what counts as evidence that the loop is actually functioning rather than merely producing performative consultation or metric gaming.

What are the identity conditions of an institution under recursive self-revision? The conjecture assumes an institution can change its rules and interpretations without dissolving its identity, but it does not specify what the stable core is, how it is recognized, or when adaptation becomes replacement. This is a central unresolved tension because the whole model relies on distinguishing legitimate transformation from institutional death or capture.

Under what structural and environmental conditions does increased reflexivity improve legitimacy rather than destabilize it? The conjecture treats stronger self-modeling as generally adaptive, but recursive feedback can also amplify conflict, manipulation, short-term opinion swings, or elite capture. The open question is where the threshold lies between productive strange loops and pathological self-reference.
